skill.name: multi-cloud-architecture
skill.description: Design multi-cloud architectures using a decision framework to select and integrate services across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.summary: Plan cloud-agnostic architectures, service choices, migrations, and cost strategies across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting, cloud service names, and ordinary architecture terms. No executable shell commands, weak cryptographic implementation, command-and-control behavior, prompt injection, or data exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown inline links and a fenced architecture diagram, not Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation. These locations contain documentation references and a text diagram only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are all Markdown syntax or documentation references. There is no command interpreter, script, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: Cloud Terms Misclassified as Malware or Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The high-severity alerts are keyword collisions in benign architecture prose. The C2 alert maps to EC2, and weak crypto alerts map to words such as Design and cloud-agnostic rather than cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is cloud architecture guidance, not cryptography or command-and-control infrastructure. No malicious behavior or implementation code is present.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to compare AWS, Azure, and GCP services without creating vendor lock-in. This skill provides a practical framework for service selection, migration planning, cost optimization, and resilient architecture design.
actual_capabilities: Compares common AWS, Azure, and GCP compute, storage, and database services.
Explains multi-cloud patterns such as disaster recovery, best-of-breed services, geographic distribution, and cloud abstraction.
Recommends cloud-agnostic building blocks such as Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis, object storage, and service mesh tools.
Outlines phased migration steps from assessment through pilot, migration, and optimization.
Identifies cost optimization strategies for reserved capacity, spot capacity, right-sizing, lifecycle policies, and cost allocation tags.
limitations: It does not provide current cloud pricing or region availability.
It does not deploy infrastructure or execute provider commands.
It does not replace provider-specific security, compliance, or legal review.
It does not include detailed implementation files in the scanned marketplace package.

use_cases.title: Plan a Resilient Multi-Cloud Strategy
use_cases.description: Define primary and recovery cloud roles, replication needs, failover expectations, and provider boundaries.

use_cases.title: Compare Provider Services for a Workload
use_cases.description: Map workload requirements to equivalent compute, storage, database, cache, queue, and monitoring services.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Cloud Migration Roadmap
use_cases.description: Break a migration into assessment, pilot, migration, and optimization phases with validation checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Basic Cloud Services
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare AWS, Azure, and GCP options for compute, object storage, and managed SQL. Keep the answer concise.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Multi-Cloud Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend a multi-cloud pattern for an application with strict uptime, moderate latency needs, and limited platform staff.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a phased migration plan from one cloud provider to a multi-cloud design. Include risks, validation, and rollback points.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Cloud-Agnostic Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a cloud-agnostic platform using Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis, object storage, monitoring, and service mesh components.

output_examples.input: Compare managed container options across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
output_examples.output: A concise comparison of ECS, Azure Container Instances, Cloud Run, EKS, AKS, GKE, and managed container tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Design a disaster recovery pattern across two providers.
output_examples.output: A primary-provider architecture with secondary-provider recovery, database replication, failover automation, monitoring, and recovery testing steps.

output_examples.input: Plan a multi-cloud migration for a monolithic application.
output_examples.output: A four-phase roadmap covering assessment, pilot workload selection, incremental migration, dual-run validation, and post-migration optimization.

best_practices: Start with business goals, compliance needs, latency targets, and operational capacity before selecting providers.
Use portable foundations such as infrastructure as code, Kubernetes, standard databases, and open monitoring tools when portability matters.
Test failover, recovery, cost controls, and team operations before relying on a multi-cloud production design.
anti_patterns: Do not use multiple providers only to appear cloud-neutral without a clear resilience, cost, compliance, or capability reason.
Do not assume equivalent provider services have identical limits, pricing, security controls, or operational behavior.
Do not split tightly coupled application components across clouds without measuring latency, data transfer cost, and failure modes.

faq.question: What cloud providers does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers AWS, Azure, and GCP service selection and architecture patterns.

faq.question: Can it help avoid vendor lock-in?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests cloud-agnostic services and abstraction layers when portability is a requirement.

faq.question: Does it calculate exact cloud costs?
faq.answer: No. It explains cost factors and optimization strategies, but it does not provide live pricing.

faq.question: Does it deploy infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and planning steps, not deployment automation.

faq.question: Is it useful for disaster recovery planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a single-provider primary pattern with disaster recovery in another cloud.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
